_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Mental confusion / Disorientation/ Delirium or coma
New onset of seizures (excluding simple febrile seizures)

ppd_preauth.q: Does the clinical note mention acute onset of fever (</= 5 – 7 days) ?
Did the clinical note mention altered sensorium and onset of seizures?

common_queries: Acute onset of fever not documented or outside the 5-7 days window in clinical notes
Altered sensorium and/or onset of seizures not documented in clinical notes
CSF examination not performed or not documented at claim stage
Discharge summary not available at claim stage
Clinical notes with indications not provided at pre-authorization
Chest X Ray not provided at pre-authorization
Planned line of treatment not provided at pre-authorization
Indoor case papers not provided at claim stage
CT Brain not provided at claim stage

clinical_key_pointers: Mental confusion / Disorientation/ Delirium or coma
additional_information.post_op_care: Parents have been explained the supportive care and physiotherapy to be continued at home
additional_information.fitness_criteria: Hemodynamically stable; Improvement in consciousness; Afebrile; Has started eating and drinking orally; Seizures have subsided
additional_information.referral_criteria: If any of the following signs are present, the child should be referred to tertiary care facility with PICU and facilities for mechanical ventilation: Glasgow Coma Scale < 8, Abnormal breathing pattern, Shock not responding to fluid bolus, Decerebrate or decorticate posturing, Seizures persisting despite benzodiazepine and phenytoin
contraindications: Lumbar puncture is contra-indicated or neuroimaging must be obtained before lumbar puncture if: Fundus papilledema, Platelet count < 50,000, Focal neurological deficits, Asymmetric/unreactive pupils, Decerebrate/decorticate posturing
